section.title: Overview
section.para: The OpenNLP SpellChecker is a native, language-agnostic spelling corrector built on the SymSpell (Symmetric Delete) algorithm. Given a (possibly misspelled) term it proposes corrections drawn from a frequency dictionary, ranked by edit distance and corpus frequency. It can also correct whole phrases, repairing wrongly inserted or omitted spaces (word splits and merges) with the help of a bigram language model.

SymSpell is fast because it pre-computes a deletes-only index: for every dictionary term it derives all strings obtained by deleting up to a small number of symbols and maps each such delete back to the originating terms. A query is answered by generating the deletes of the query and intersecting them with the index, which turns the costly fuzzy search into a handful of hash-map lookups. Candidates are then verified with an edit-distance metric. Because only deletes are generated (no insertions, substitutions or transpositions), candidate generation is orders of magnitude cheaper than a naïve search over the whole dictionary.

note.para: The algorithm and its compound-correction heuristic are an independent Java rewrite of the SymSpell reference implementation by Wolf Garbe (MIT). Attribution is recorded in the project NOTICE file.

section.title: SpellChecker Tools
section.para: The extension provides two command line tools, dispatched through the bin/spellcheck launcher of the binary distribution: SpellCheckModelBuilder builds a binary model from plain-text frequency dictionaries, and CorrectText corrects text using such a model. Each tool prints its usage when invoked with the help parameter.

section.title: Building a Model
section.para:
A model is built from a unigram frequency list and, optionally, a bigram list (see for the file format). Usage of the tool:
$ bin/spellcheck SpellCheckModelBuilder help Usage: opennlp-spellcheck SpellCheckModelBuilder -unigrams in [-bigrams in] -model out \ [-maxEditDistance num] [-countThreshold num] [-distance damerau-osa|levenshtein] \ [-corpusWordCount num] -lang lang [-prefixLength num] [-encoding charsetName]
Arguments description: -lang lang The language tag of the model (e.g. en), stored as model.language. -unigrams in The unigram frequency dictionary file (word<TAB>count per line). -bigrams in An optional bigram frequency dictionary file (w1 w2<TAB>count per line). -model out The output file the binary SymSpell model is written to. -maxEditDistance num The largest precomputed dictionary edit distance. (default 2) -prefixLength num Number of leading symbols used for delete generation (must be > maxEditDistance). (default 7) -countThreshold num Minimum corpus count for a term to be indexed. (default 1) -distance damerau-osa|levenshtein The edit-distance metric used for verification. (default damerau-osa) -corpusWordCount num Corpus size N used for compound-correction scoring; 0 (default) derives N from the dictionary's summed counts. -encoding charsetName encoding for reading and writing text, if absent the system default is used.
The following command builds an English model from a unigram and a bigram dictionary, using the default Damerau-OSA edit distance:
$ bin/spellcheck SpellCheckModelBuilder -lang en \ -unigrams en-unigrams.txt -bigrams en-bigrams.txt \ -model en-spellcheck.bin -encoding UTF-8

section.title: Correcting Text
section.para:
Once a model has been built (or a packaged model is on the classpath), the CorrectText tool corrects text line by line. Usage of the tool:
$ bin/spellcheck CorrectText help Usage: opennlp-spellcheck CorrectText [-model model] [-maxEditDistance num] [-lang lang] \ [-suggest true|false] [-verbosity TOP|CLOSEST|ALL] [-compound true|false] \ [-inputFile in] [-outputFile out] [-encoding charsetName]
Arguments description: -model model A binary SymSpell model file. Either -model or -lang must be given. -lang lang Resolve a packaged spellcheck model for this language from the classpath. Either -model or -lang must be given. -inputFile in The input text file (read line by line). If absent, standard input is used. -outputFile out The output text file. If absent, standard output is used. -compound true|false Correct each line as a whole phrase, repairing wrong word splits/merges. (default false) -maxEditDistance num The maximum edit distance considered per token. (default 2) -suggest true|false List the suggestions for each token (honoring -verbosity) instead of emitting corrected text. (default false) -verbosity TOP|CLOSEST|ALL How many suggestions to list per token; only used with -suggest true. TOP=best only, CLOSEST=all at the smallest distance, ALL=all within -maxEditDistance. (default TOP) -encoding charsetName encoding for reading and writing text, if absent the system default is used.
By default each line is corrected token by token, reading from standard input and writing to standard output:
$ echo "the qick brwn fox" | bin/spellcheck CorrectText -model en-spellcheck.bin the quick brown fox
With -compound true the whole line is corrected as a phrase, additionally repairing wrongly inserted or omitted spaces:
$ echo "thequick brownfox" | bin/spellcheck CorrectText -model en-spellcheck.bin -compound true the quick brown fox
With -suggest true the tool lists the candidate suggestions for every token (honoring -verbosity) instead of emitting corrected text:
$ echo "teh qick" | bin/spellcheck CorrectText -model en-spellcheck.bin -suggest true -verbosity CLOSEST teh => [the] qick => [quick, quack]
Instead of an explicit -model file you can resolve a packaged model from the classpath by language with -lang (see ):
$ bin/spellcheck CorrectText -lang en -inputFile noisy.txt -outputFile clean.txt -encoding UTF-8

section.title: SpellChecker API
Choosing an Edit Distance
section.para:
The verification metric is pluggable behind the EditDistance interface and selected through SymSpellConfig. Two implementations are provided:
DamerauOSADistance (the default) – an Optimal String Alignment (restricted Damerau-Levenshtein) metric in which a transposition of two adjacent symbols counts as a single edit. It is Unicode-aware, operating on code points, and matches the variant used by the SymSpell reference implementation. Prefer it for natural-language text where transpositions ("teh" → "the") are common typos.
LevenshteinDistance – a thin adapter over Apache Commons Text in which only insertions, deletions and substitutions count, so a transposition costs two edits. It operates on UTF-16 char units. Use it when you want plain Levenshtein semantics or to match another system's behavior.
Both expose a shared, stateless INSTANCE and are passed to the builder:
SymSpellConfig config = SymSpellConfig.builder() .editDistance(LevenshteinDistance.INSTANCE) .build();

section.title: Dictionary and Model Format
section.para: A SpellChecker is driven by plain-text frequency dictionaries, loaded into an engine by the FrequencyDictionaryLoader. Two UTF-8 (by default) line-based formats are supported. In both, columns are separated by a single TAB; blank lines, whitespace-only lines and lines starting with # (comments) are skipped.

The unigram dictionary lists one word<TAB>count per line, where count is the term's corpus frequency:
the 23135851162 of 13151942776 and 12997637966 quick 1885979 brown 1019234 fox 271289
The optional bigram dictionary lists one word1 word2<TAB>count per line (the two words separated by a space, then a TAB and the count). Bigrams improve compound correction:
quick brown 12046 brown fox 5634 the quick 98765

section.title: Multi-language Models
section.para: Production multi-language dictionaries are large and are therefore not bundled in the opennlp-spellcheck jar. Instead, each language ships as a separate Maven artifact named opennlp-models-spellcheck-{lang} (for example opennlp-models-spellcheck-en), packaged as a binary model plus a matching model.properties descriptor (model.language, model.name, model.version, model.sha256).

Add the model artifact for the language you need to your classpath:
<dependency> <groupId>org.apache.opennlp</groupId> <artifactId>opennlp-models-spellcheck-en</artifactId> <version>CURRENT_OPENNLP_VERSION</version> </dependency>
The SymSpellModelResolver then discovers and loads the matching model using the standard OpenNLP model-resolver machinery (see ). It returns an Optional that is empty when no matching model jar is present:
Optional<SymSpellModel> model = new SymSpellModelResolver().resolveByLanguage("en"); SpellChecker checker = model.orElseThrow().getSymSpell(); List<SuggestItem> suggestions = checker.lookup("qick");
On the command line the same resolution happens when you pass -lang instead of -model to CorrectText.

section.title: Pipeline Integration
section.para: The SpellChecker integrates into OpenNLP preprocessing through the standard normalizer and stream abstractions, so correction can be slotted into an existing chain without bespoke glue code.
